tasks.text: Optimize rayAABB_inv by reducing min/max dependency chain depth
Optimize BVH traversal in IntersectBVH to reduce memory traffic by loading BVHNode data more efficiently
Optimize RayBoxItersect calls in SampleEmission by precomputing invDir/bias and using rayAABB_inv
Replace RayBoxItersect in SampleEmission with precomputed bias/invDir and rayAABB_inv
SSE vectorize rayAABB_inv to reduce per-call latency

notes.text: rayAABB_inv is the #1 hotspot at 29.7% exclusive time. It's a simple branchless slab test with precomputed bias/invRd. The function is heavily called from IntersectBVH traversal. Potential optimizations: (1) manually unroll the 3 components to reduce fminf/fmaxf overhead, (2) use SIMD intrinsics, (3) restructure to reduce register pressure.

Let me try a more aggressive approach: use a flatter min/max tree to reduce dependency depth from 3 operations to 2 operations. Also try to coerce the compiler into using minss/maxss instructions by avoiding fminf/fmaxf.

The modest improvement on rayAABB_inv suggests it's already quite optimized. Next target: IntersectBVH traversal - the stack is stored as int[64] but we could use a more efficient stack approach. Also notice that IntersectBVH pushes both children and we check rayAABB_inv twice per internal node. We could avoid the redundant float3 loads of BBmin/BBmax.
Need to restore after regressed IntersectBVH change. Going to focus on rayAABB_inv optimization which gave a real improvement, and then look at avoiding redundant work in the BVH traversal.

The current rayAABB_inv has a 3-deep dependency chain for tmin/tmax. By restructuring as a binary min/max tree we can reduce to 2 levels. Also, the && comparison at the end can be replaced with a bitwise trick: compute tmin > tmax via sign bit.
rayTriangle is now the #1 hotspot at 19.8% exclusive. Attempting manual scalarization of the MT algorithm to reduce register spills and improve scheduling.

SampleEmission loops over all objects calling RayBoxItersect, which recomputes 1/d per axis each time. Precompute invDir and bias once, then use rayAABB_inv directly to save divisions and branch overhead in the AABB test.
